Saengchai Phochana.desc: Live mud crabs and Thai-Chinese seafood. Classic go-to after a night out.
Saengchai Phochana.meta: 762/5-7 Sukhumvit Rd, Khlong Tan, Khlong Toei 10110

Pier 21 Food Court (Terminal 21).desc: Cheap, well-run Thai food court. Great intro to the classics — pad krapao, green curry, stir-fries.
Pier 21 Food Court (Terminal 21).meta: Level 5, Terminal 21, 88 Sukhumvit Rd, Khlong Toei Nuea, Watthana 10110

Rung Rueang Pork Noodle.desc: Michelin-listed tom yum pork noodle soup (70฿). 6+ years in the Guide.

Wattana Panich.desc: 40-year-old simmered Chinese-style beef noodle broth (100฿). One of Bangkok's most iconic bowls.

Phed Mark.desc: Mark Wiens' restaurant — VERY spicy pad krapao. For chili lovers.

Baan E-sarn Muangyos.desc: Isan-style cooking in a pub atmosphere — food + beers. Don't overdo it on the salad for dinner.

Little Bee Thai Crispy Crepe.desc: Made-to-order Thai crispy crepes (khanom buang). Michelin Bib 2021–2024. Try the Sweet Golden Egg Yolk Threads. Est. 1984.

Ann Guay Tiew Kua Gai.desc: Fried rice noodles with chicken and egg (60฿). 5 years in the Michelin Guide.

Nai Ek Roll Noodle.desc: Clear Chinese-style pork noodle soup with crispy pork belly (100฿). 5 years in the Michelin Guide.

Kuay Jab Mr Joe.desc: Peppery rolled rice-noodle soup with crispy pork belly.

Kor Panich (K. Panich).desc: Legendary mango sticky rice — 130฿. 7 years in the Michelin Guide. Pair with a temple visit.

Thipsamai Pad Thai.desc: The most famous pad thai in Thailand — secret weapon is prawn oil. Try the Pad Thai Song-Kreung (ultimate version).
Thipsamai Pad Thai.meta: 313–315 Maha Chai Rd, Samran Rat, Phra Nakhon 10200

Original Pad Kra Pao 1993.desc: Pad krapao done the traditional way. Very affordable (~8–20 ILS/dish).

Shu Daxia Hot Pot.desc: Chinese-style hot pot with marbled beef rolls. Good group dinner.
Shu Daxia Hot Pot.meta: Floor 6, MBK Center, 444 Phaya Thai Rd, Pathum Wan 10330

Kam's Roast.desc: 1 Michelin Star for 11 years. Hong Kong–style roast duck (350฿ / quarter).

Jeh O Chula.desc: Legendary late-night Mama tom yum noodles. Expect a queue — arrive early.

Jae Wan.desc: Local Thai comfort food — a neighborhood favorite.

Sook Siam Market.desc: Giant river prawns and regional Thai food stalls under one roof. A must for market lovers.

Somboon Seafood.desc: Bangkok's most famous curry crab (pu phad pong karee). Tourist-heavy but the crab is legit.

Heng Chun Seng.desc: Best beef soup in Bangkok. Order the hot pot soup with the mixed-meat plate.

Ginger Farm Kitchen.desc: Farm-to-city Thai, zero MSG. Michelin Bib Gourmand 2020–2023. Convenient stop on arrival or departure.

Kin Kin Seafood.desc: Mama Tom Yum noodles with raw egg yolks and crab fried rice. Pricey for Bangkok but quality is 10/10.

Uncle Liu.desc: Thai-Chinese fusion — stir-fries, fried rice with egg. Very local, hidden gem.

Good Taste Street Food.desc: Thai classics — pad krapao, grilled pork, stir-fried morning glory. Solid repeat-visit spot.

Lay Lao.desc: Comforting Hua Hin–style Thai. Michelin Bib Gourmand 2018–2025. Try the Deep Fried Sun Dried Pork Belly (185฿) and the whole young coconut.

Bamee Kon Sae Lee Thong Lo.desc: Classic Sino-Thai egg noodle shop — crispy pork, wontons, dry or soup. Cheap, decades old, queues at dinner.

Kim Jeong Grill.desc: Korean BBQ done right — marbled wagyu, banchan, the full smoky table-grill experience. Book ahead.

Jodd Fairs Ratchada.desc: Huge open-air night market for street-food grazing. Go hungry — volcano pork ribs, seafood, hundreds of stalls.
Jodd Fairs Ratchada.meta: Rama IX, Huai Khwang (MRT Thailand Cultural Centre)
